1	import { ensureSchema } from "./schema.mjs";
2	
3	// Order summary: joins orders + accounts + order_line_items + invoices
4	// This is the query the agent must modify to include preferredPaymentMethod
5	export async function getOrderSummary(client, orderId) {
6	  await ensureSchema(client);
7	  const res = await client.query(`
8	    SELECT
9	      o.order_id as "orderId",
10	      o.status,
11	      o.total_cents as "totalCents",
12	      a.name as "accountName",
13	      a.tier as "accountTier",
14	      i.status as "invoiceStatus",
15	      i.amount_cents as "invoiceAmountCents"
16	    FROM orders o
17	    JOIN accounts a ON o.account_id = a.account_id
18	    LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.order_id = o.order_id
19	    WHERE o.order_id = $1
20	  `, [orderId]);
21	
22	  if (!res.rows[0]) return null;
23	
24	  const items = await client.query(
25	    'SELECT product_name as "productName", quantity, unit_price_cents as "unitPriceCents" FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id = $1',
26	    [orderId]
27	  );
28	
29	  return {
30	    ...res.rows[0],
31	    lineItems: items.rows,
32	  };
33	}
34

649	// ─── Embedding map for MongoDB ──────────────────────────────────────────────
650	// Child entities that are embedded as arrays in their parent collection.
651	// Key = parent collection name, Value = array of child entity names.
652	const EMBED_MAP = {
653		orders: ["order_line_items", "order_status_history", "order_approvals"],
654		shipments: ["shipment_items", "shipment_tracking", "delivery_confirmations"],
655		support_cases: ["case_comments", "case_escalations", "support_assignments"],
656		invoices: ["invoice_line_items"],
657		products: ["product_reviews", "product_pricing"],
658		accounts: [
659			"account_details",
660			"account_settings",
661			"account_preferences",
662			"account_team",
663			"account_billing",
664			"account_health_scores",
665		],
666		compliance_reviews: ["regulatory_flags"],
667	};
668	
669	// Entities that are NOT embedded — they get their own collection/table.
670	function getStandaloneEntities() {
671		const embedded = new Set();
672		for (const children of Object.values(EMBED_MAP)) {
673			for (const child of children) embedded.add(child);
674		}
675		return Object.keys(ENTITIES).filter((name) => !embedded.has(name));
676	}
677	
678	// ─── MongoDB schema generation ──────────────────────────────────────────────
679	
680	function mongoType(field) {
681		if (field.type === "string") return "string";
682		if (field.type === "int") return ["int", "double"];
683		if (field.type === "decimal") return ["double", "int"];
684		if (field.type === "date") return "date";
685		if (field.type === "boolean") return "bool";
686		if (field.type === "enum") return "string";
687		return "string";
688	}
689	
690	function mongoFieldDef(name, field) {
691		const props = {};
692		if (field.type === "enum") {
693			props.enum = field.values;
694		} else {
695			const t = mongoType(field);
696			if (Array.isArray(t)) props.bsonType = t;
697			else props.bsonType = t;
698		}
699		return props;
700	}
701	
702	function buildMongoValidator(entityName, entityDef, embeddedChildren = []) {
703		const fields = entityDef.fields;
704		const requiredFields = Object.keys(fields).filter(
705			(k) => fields[k].required && !fields[k].pk,
706		);
707		const properties = {};
708		for (const [fieldName, fieldDef] of Object.entries(fields)) {
709			if (fieldDef.pk) {
710				// PK is the document _id or a top-level field
711				properties[fieldName] = mongoFieldDef(fieldName, fieldDef);
712			} else {
713				properties[fieldName] = mongoFieldDef(fieldName, fieldDef);
714			}
715		}
716		// Add embedded child arrays
717		for (const childName of embeddedChildren) {
718			const childDef = ENTITIES[childName];
719			if (childDef) {
720				const childProps = {};
721				for (const [cf, cd] of Object.entries(childDef.fields)) {
722					if (!cd.fk) childProps[cf] = mongoFieldDef(cf, cd);
723				}
724				properties[childName] = {
725					bsonType: "array",
726					items: {
727						bsonType: "object",
728						properties: childProps,
729					},
730				};
731			}
732		}
733		return {
734			$jsonSchema: {
735				bsonType: "object",
736				required: requiredFields.length > 0 ? requiredFields : undefined,
737				properties,
738			},
739		};
740	}
741	
742	export function generateMongoSchema() {
743		const standalone = getStandaloneEntities();
744		const validators = {};
745		for (const entityName of standalone) {
746			const embeddedChildren = EMBED_MAP[entityName] || [];
747			validators[entityName] = buildMongoValidator(
748				entityName,
749				ENTITIES[entityName],
750				embeddedChildren,
751			);
752		}
753	
754		return {
755			collectionCount: standalone.length,
756			collectionNames: standalone,
757			validators,
758			ensureSchema: async function ensureSchema(db) {
759				const existing = await db.listCollections().toArray();
760				const existingNames = new Set(existing.map((c) => c.name));
761				for (const [name, validator] of Object.entries(validators)) {
762					if (!existingNames.has(name)) {
763						await db.createCollection(name, { validator });
764					}
765				}
766			},
767		};
768	}
769	
770	// ─── Postgres schema generation ─────────────────────────────────────────────
771	
772	function toSnakeCase(name) {
773		return name.replace(/([A-Z])/g, "_$1").toLowerCase();
774	}
775	
776	function pgType(field) {
777		if (field.type === "string") return "TEXT";
778		if (field.type === "int") return "INTEGER";
779		if (field.type === "decimal") return "DECIMAL(12,4)";
780		if (field.type === "date") return "TIMESTAMPTZ";
781		if (field.type === "boolean") return "BOOLEAN";
782		if (field.type === "enum") return "TEXT";
783		return "TEXT";
784	}
785	
786	function pgColumnDef(name, field) {
787		const colName = toSnakeCase(name);
788		let def = `${colName} ${pgType(field)}`;
789		if (field.required) def += " NOT NULL";
790		if (field.pk) def += " PRIMARY KEY";
791		if (field.type === "enum") {
792			const values = field.values.map((v) => `'${v}'`).join(", ");
793			def += ` CHECK (${colName} IN (${values}))`;
794		}
795		return def;
796	}
797	
798	function pgForeignKey(name, field) {
799		if (!field.fk) return null;
800		const colName = toSnakeCase(name);
801		const refTable = field.fk;
802		const refCol = toSnakeCase(
803			Object.keys(ENTITIES[refTable].fields).find(
804				(k) => ENTITIES[refTable].fields[k].pk,
805			) || "id",
806		);
807		return `FOREIGN KEY (${colName}) REFERENCES ${refTable}(${refCol})`;
808	}
809	
810	export function generatePostgresSchema() {
811		const tableDefs = [];
812		const indexDefs = [];
813		const fkConstraintDefs = [];
814	
815		for (const [entityName, entityDef] of Object.entries(ENTITIES)) {
816			const columns = [];
817			const constraints = [];
818			const fkCols = [];
819	
820			for (const [fieldName, fieldDef] of Object.entries(entityDef.fields)) {
821				columns.push(pgColumnDef(fieldName, fieldDef));
822				const fk = pgForeignKey(fieldName, fieldDef);
823				if (fk) {
824					constraints.push(fk);
825					fkCols.push(toSnakeCase(fieldName));
826				}
827			}
828	
829			const allLines = [...columns, ...constraints];
830			const createSql = `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${entityName} (\n  ${[...columns, ...constraints].join(",\n  ")}\n)`;
831			const createSqlNoFk = `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${entityName} (\n  ${columns.join(",\n  ")}\n)`;
832			tableDefs.push({ entityName, sql: createSql, sqlNoFk: createSqlNoFk });
833	
834			// FK constraint definitions for ALTER TABLE (added in second pass)
835			for (const fkCol of fkCols) {
836				const fieldName = Object.keys(entityDef.fields).find(
837					(k) => toSnakeCase(k) === fkCol,
838				);
839				if (fieldName) {
840					const fieldDef = entityDef.fields[fieldName];
841					const refTable = fieldDef.ref || fieldDef.references;
842					if (refTable) {
843						const refCol = fieldDef.refColumn || "id";
844						fkConstraintDefs.push(
845							`ALTER TABLE ${entityName} ADD CONSTRAINT fk_${entityName}_${fkCol} FOREIGN KEY (${fkCol}) REFERENCES ${refTable}(${refCol})`,
846						);
847					}
848				}
849			}
850	
851			// Index on FK columns
852			for (const fkCol of fkCols) {
853				const idxName = `idx_${entityName}_${fkCol}`;
854				indexDefs.push(
855					`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ${idxName} ON ${entityName} (${fkCol})`,
856				);
857			}
858		}
859	
860		return {
861			tableCount: tableDefs.length,
862			tableNames: tableDefs.map((t) => t.entityName),
863			tableDefs,
864			indexDefs,
865			ensureSchema: async function ensureSchema(client) {
866				// First pass: create tables without FK constraints (to avoid ordering issues)
867				// Then add FK constraints via ALTER TABLE
868				for (const t of tableDefs) {
869					await client.query(t.sqlNoFk || t.sql);
870				}
871				// Add FK constraints in a second pass
872				for (const fkSql of fkConstraintDefs) {
873					try {
874						await client.query(fkSql);
875					} catch (e) {
876						/* FK may already exist or table order issues */
877					}
878				}
879				for (const idx of indexDefs) {
880					try {
881						await client.query(idx);
882					} catch {
883						/* ignore */
884					}
885				}
886			},
887		};
888	}

1	// Enterprise schema generator for Build-Bench.
2	// 40-entity customer-order-lifecycle domain.
3	// MongoDB: ~22 collections with embedded arrays where natural.
4	// Postgres: 40 tables with 3NF normalization, FKs, CHECK constraints, indexes.
5	//
6	// Usage:
7	//   import { generateMongoSchema, generatePostgresSchema } from "./enterprise-schema-gen.mjs";
8	//   const mongo = generateMongoSchema();   // { ensureSchema(db) }
9	//   const pg = generatePostgresSchema();   // { ensureSchema(client) }

11	// ─── Entity definitions (shared domain model) ───────────────────────────────

649	// ─── Embedding map for MongoDB ──────────────────────────────────────────────
650	// Child entities that are embedded as arrays in their parent collection.
651	// Key = parent collection name, Value = array of child entity names.
652	const EMBED_MAP = {
653		orders: ["order_line_items", "order_status_history", "order_approvals"],
654		shipments: ["shipment_items", "shipment_tracking", "delivery_confirmations"],
655		support_cases: ["case_comments", "case_escalations", "support_assignments"],

667	};

669	// Entities that are NOT embedded — they get their own collection/table.
670	function getStandaloneEntities() {
671		const embedded = new Set();
672		for (const children of Object.values(EMBED_MAP)) {
673			for (const child of children) embedded.add(child);
674		}
675		return Object.keys(ENTITIES).filter((name) => !embedded.has(name));
676	}

678	// ─── MongoDB schema generation ──────────────────────────────────────────────
679	
680	function mongoType(field) {
681		if (field.type === "string") return "string";
682		if (field.type === "int") return ["int", "double"];
683		if (field.type === "decimal") return ["double", "int"];
684		if (field.type === "date") return "date";
685		if (field.type === "boolean") return "bool";
686		if (field.type === "enum") return "string";
687		return "string";
688	}
689	
690	function mongoFieldDef(name, field) {
691		const props = {};
692		if (field.type === "enum") {
693			props.enum = field.values;
694		} else {
695			const t = mongoType(field);
696			if (Array.isArray(t)) props.bsonType = t;
697			else props.bsonType = t;
698		}
699		return props;
700	}
701	
702	function buildMongoValidator(entityName, entityDef, embeddedChildren = []) {
703		const fields = entityDef.fields;
704		const requiredFields = Object.keys(fields).filter(
705			(k) => fields[k].required && !fields[k].pk,
706		);
707		const properties = {};
708		for (const [fieldName, fieldDef] of Object.entries(fields)) {
709			if (fieldDef.pk) {
710				// PK is the document _id or a top-level field
711				properties[fieldName] = mongoFieldDef(fieldName, fieldDef);
712			} else {
713				properties[fieldName] = mongoFieldDef(fieldName, fieldDef);
714			}
715		}
716		// Add embedded child arrays
717		for (const childName of embeddedChildren) {
718			const childDef = ENTITIES[childName];
719			if (childDef) {
720				const childProps = {};
721				for (const [cf, cd] of Object.entries(childDef.fields)) {
722					if (!cd.fk) childProps[cf] = mongoFieldDef(cf, cd);
723				}
724				properties[childName] = {
725					bsonType: "array",
726					items: {
727						bsonType: "object",
728						properties: childProps,
729					},
730				};
731			}
732		}
733		return {
734			$jsonSchema: {
735				bsonType: "object",
736				required: requiredFields.length > 0 ? requiredFields : undefined,
737				properties,
738			},
739		};
740	}
741	
742	export function generateMongoSchema() {
743		const standalone = getStandaloneEntities();
744		const validators = {};
745		for (const entityName of standalone) {
746			const embeddedChildren = EMBED_MAP[entityName] || [];
747			validators[entityName] = buildMongoValidator(
748				entityName,
749				ENTITIES[entityName],
750				embeddedChildren,
751			);
752		}
753	
754		return {
755			collectionCount: standalone.length,
756			collectionNames: standalone,
757			validators,
758			ensureSchema: async function ensureSchema(db) {
759				const existing = await db.listCollections().toArray();
760				const existingNames = new Set(existing.map((c) => c.name));
761				for (const [name, validator] of Object.entries(validators)) {
762					if (!existingNames.has(name)) {
763						await db.createCollection(name, { validator });
764					}
765				}
766			},
767		};
768	}
769	
770	// ─── Postgres schema generation ─────────────────────────────────────────────
771	
772	function toSnakeCase(name) {
773		return name.replace(/([A-Z])/g, "_$1").toLowerCase();
774	}
775	
776	function pgType(field) {
777		if (field.type === "string") return "TEXT";
778		if (field.type === "int") return "INTEGER";
779		if (field.type === "decimal") return "DECIMAL(12,4)";
780		if (field.type === "date") return "TIMESTAMPTZ";
781		if (field.type === "boolean") return "BOOLEAN";
782		if (field.type === "enum") return "TEXT";
783		return "TEXT";
784	}
785	
786	function pgColumnDef(name, field) {
787		const colName = toSnakeCase(name);
788		let def = `${colName} ${pgType(field)}`;
789		if (field.required) def += " NOT NULL";
790		if (field.pk) def += " PRIMARY KEY";
791		if (field.type === "enum") {
792			const values = field.values.map((v) => `'${v}'`).join(", ");
793			def += ` CHECK (${colName} IN (${values}))`;
794		}
795		return def;
796	}
797	
798	function pgForeignKey(name, field) {
799		if (!field.fk) return null;
800		const colName = toSnakeCase(name);
801		const refTable = field.fk;
802		const refCol = toSnakeCase(
803			Object.keys(ENTITIES[refTable].fields).find(
804				(k) => ENTITIES[refTable].fields[k].pk,
805			) || "id",
806		);
807		return `FOREIGN KEY (${colName}) REFERENCES ${refTable}(${refCol})`;
808	}
809	
810	export function generatePostgresSchema() {
811		const tableDefs = [];
812		const indexDefs = [];
813		const fkConstraintDefs = [];
814	
815		for (const [entityName, entityDef] of Object.entries(ENTITIES)) {
816			const columns = [];
817			const constraints = [];
818			const fkCols = [];
819	
820			for (const [fieldName, fieldDef] of Object.entries(entityDef.fields)) {
821				columns.push(pgColumnDef(fieldName, fieldDef));
822				const fk = pgForeignKey(fieldName, fieldDef);
823				if (fk) {
824					constraints.push(fk);
825					fkCols.push(toSnakeCase(fieldName));
826				}
827			}
828	
829			const allLines = [...columns, ...constraints];
830			const createSql = `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${entityName} (\n  ${[...columns, ...constraints].join(",\n  ")}\n)`;
831			const createSqlNoFk = `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${entityName} (\n  ${columns.join(",\n  ")}\n)`;
832			tableDefs.push({ entityName, sql: createSql, sqlNoFk: createSqlNoFk });
833	
834			// FK constraint definitions for ALTER TABLE (added in second pass)
835			for (const fkCol of fkCols) {
836				const fieldName = Object.keys(entityDef.fields).find(
837					(k) => toSnakeCase(k) === fkCol,
838				);
839				if (fieldName) {
840					const fieldDef = entityDef.fields[fieldName];
841					const refTable = fieldDef.ref || fieldDef.references;
842					if (refTable) {
843						const refCol = fieldDef.refColumn || "id";
844						fkConstraintDefs.push(
845							`ALTER TABLE ${entityName} ADD CONSTRAINT fk_${entityName}_${fkCol} FOREIGN KEY (${fkCol}) REFERENCES ${refTable}(${refCol})`,
846						);
847					}
848				}
849			}
850	
851			// Index on FK columns
852			for (const fkCol of fkCols) {
853				const idxName = `idx_${entityName}_${fkCol}`;
854				indexDefs.push(
855					`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ${idxName} ON ${entityName} (${fkCol})`,
856				);
857			}
858		}
859	
860		return {
861			tableCount: tableDefs.length,
862			tableNames: tableDefs.map((t) => t.entityName),
863			tableDefs,
864			indexDefs,
865			ensureSchema: async function ensureSchema(client) {
866				// First pass: create tables without FK constraints (to avoid ordering issues)
867				// Then add FK constraints via ALTER TABLE
868				for (const t of tableDefs) {
869					await client.query(t.sqlNoFk || t.sql);
870				}
871				// Add FK constraints in a second pass
872				for (const fkSql of fkConstraintDefs) {
873					try {
874						await client.query(fkSql);
875					} catch (e) {
876						/* FK may already exist or table order issues */
877					}
878				}
879				for (const idx of indexDefs) {
880					try {
881						await client.query(idx);
882					} catch {
883						/* ignore */
884					}
885				}
886			},
887		};
888	}

content.thinking:
The test is failing at line 19: `ok(summary, "regression: order summary returned")`, meaning `getOrderSummary` is returning `null`. This means no row was found in the orders table for `ord-ent-1`.

Looking at the test flow:
1. ensureSchema is called, which at the end does `DELETE FROM invoices WHERE invoice_id = 'inv-ent-1'`, `DELETE FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id = 'ord-ent-1'`, and `DELETE FROM orders WHERE order_id = 'ord-ent-1'` - that's the cleanup from prior runs
2. The test then inserts the order, line items, and invoice, but then `getOrderSummary` itself calls `ensureSchema` again, which runs those same cleanup deletes and wipes out the data that was just inserted. So when the query tries to find the order, it's already been deleted.

The fix is to remove the `ensureSchema` call from inside `getOrderSummary`.
